course_description:
This course offers an individually designed program that enables students to gain hands-on experience in the practical application of communication principles and processes in a work environment. Students undertake short-term or part-time assignments with a host organisation of their choice. Tasks to be involved in will need to apply and build on the knowledge and skills gained from the Field of Study courses, for instance, creating a communication strategy or campaign, facilitating stakeholder engagement, creating relevant policy briefs, writing a research paper, developing project proposals, conducting a participatory situation analysis, capacity building, or reviewing existing communication practices. Suitability of proposed placements are discussed with each student individually.

The key aim of the course is to provide students with the opportunity toﾠexperience the application of their studies in a real-life work situation. Workshops to assist students to improve their employability and gain critical job-ready skills will be held throughout the course.

Although recommended, students do not have to have secured a placement before applying for COMU7015. Assistance is offered through the course administration team and all placements are checked and approved. If a student has not secured a placement before the census date, then they should drop COMU7015.

Through a short-term or part-time placement within the semester students will work with a government, non-government, community or service organisation, a commercial organisation, or any other relevant agency, on a set of tasks that build on the knowledge and skills that they gained from the core courses in their Field of Study.

Employability workshops held during the semester will focus on preparing for employment in an international job market. Students are expected to attend and participate in these workshops (unless otherwise arranged with the convenor).

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
4-8 units Field of Study courses in your Field of Study
We recommend completing the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
An introductory session is organised during Week 1 of the semester.
The placement at the host organisation involves no less than 75 hours (which approximates to two working weeks). More hours may be negotiated with the placement organisation and the course administrator (Sven Fea). The placement may be done part-time during the semester, full-time during the semester (if timetables allow), or during the break preceding the semester. Suitability of the placement and timing for undertaking the work will be discussed individually with each student. Please email the Course Coordinator jane.johnston@uq.edu.au and Course Administrator s.fea@uq.edu.au with inquiries (copy both).
course_aims: The aim of the placement course is to provide students with the opportunity to apply what they have learned in their specialist Field of Study, develop their competencies and reflect on the application of the theory and practice of their Field of Study in a specific work situation.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the application of academic learning in your specialist Field of Study to a real-life work situation.
Explain how the theory of your Field of Study can be successfully utilised in enhancing practical field applications.
Draft project documentation which is logical, coherent and relevant to clientele needs and expectations, as well as the interests and requirements of potential funding agencies.
Display an understanding of the modus operandi of a not-for-profit, commercial, government or other relevant organisation involved in strategic communication, social change or other relevant field.
Display enhanced skills in working with individuals and teams in diverse offices and/or field environments.

assessment_details.title: Workplan
assessment_details.due_date: To be submitted within 2 days after starting with the host organisation by 4pm on the relevant date.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Prepare a workplan providing brief information about the host organisation and/or project to be involved in and describing the tasks to be undertaken during the practicum. For each task the expected outcomes for the host organisation, the learning objectives for the student, and timing should be elaborated. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Machine Translation (MT) may support students in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of MT in each instance. A failure to reference MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via TurnItIn on Blackboard.
TurnItIn Receipts:
Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using TurnItIn . Before submitting any assignments for this course, you must ensure you have completed UQ's compulsory online Academic Integrity Tutorial.
When you successfully submit your assessment to TurnItIn you will see text confirming your submission is complete, before being redirected to your Assignment inbox. On this page you can:
View the name of the submitted file View date and time of the upload Resubmit your paper (if necessary) Download your submitted paper Download digital receipt.
If you cannot see your submission in your Assignment inbox you should regard your submission as unsuccessful. Students are responsible for retaining evidence of submission by the due date for all assessment items, in the required form (e.g. screenshot, email, photo, and an unaltered copy of submitted work).
If the submission was not successful:
Note the error message (preferably take a screenshot). Go to your assignment page and see if it is possible to submit again. If you cannot submit again email your course coordinator immediately with the assignment attached.
Please visit this webpage for further advice on how to submit your TurnItIn assignment .
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Reflective report
assessment_details.due_date: 22/05/2026 4:00 pm Presentation: In person in class in Week 12. Report: Submitted by 4pm Friday of Week 12.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The final report should outline the student's activities and achievements in the industry operational environment and a reflection on the experience: overview of the organisation/agency in which the practicum experience was achieved; description of the tasks undertaken and reflection onf how challenges were dealt with; outcomes achieved for the host organisation, including examples attached to the report as annexes; reflection on achievement of the learning objectives; and reflection on the overall experience and lessons learned, including successes/failures noted by the student. This is submitted at the completion of the placement in conjunction with a short oral presentation (10 minutes) to class in Week 12. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Machine Translation (MT) may support students in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of MT in each instance. A failure to reference MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Recording of Oral and Practical Assessment All presentations will be recorded for marking purposes via recording facilities available where the assessment takes place (eg. ECHO360, Zoom, camera device) Recordings will be retained by the School of Communication and Arts for at least 12 months from the release of the final grade for the course. Recordings will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed by authorised school staff for the purposes of: Moderation of marking; Provision of feedback to the student(s) recorded; and/or Re-marking following a successful re-mark application Read full task description Hide full task description

Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions are only available on the written report. If you are unable to attend the in-class presentation due to exceptional circumstances, please contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible to discuss your options.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Week 1: Introductory Session Introduction to the Industry Placement. This week, students will work with the Course Coordinator and Course Administrator to discuss, plan, and finalise their field-specific tasks for their host organisation, including the actual start/finish dates/times, number of hours of work, type of organisation, supervision arrangements, risk assessment, etc. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Week 2: In-class workshop and activities Students will continue to finalise their placement plans, plus undertake in-class and online activities that are intended to assist in the placement as well as the future career market. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Week 10: Employability Workshop 1 Two workshops will be conducted face-to-face by UQs Employability and Careers team, facilitated by the Course Coordinator. These will consider relevant topics such as networking for jobs, applying directly for jobs, applications, CVs, pitch letters, and responding to criteria-based positions. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Week 11: Employability Workshop 2 The second of the workshops to be conducted face-to-face by UQs Employability and Careers team, facilitated by the Course Coordinator. These will consider relevant topics such as networking for jobs, applying directly for jobs, applications, CVs, pitch letters, and responding to criteria-based positions. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Week 12: Presentations and Evaluation Students will share and reflect on their experiences in the meeting weeks - weeks 1, 2, 4, 5 and 12. Week 12 will be an oral presentation based on the final reflection, highlighting key learnings (5-10 minutes). Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . Course guidelines Communication Expectations While you are a student at UQ, all communication must be conducted according to the UQ Student Code of Conduct . The UQ Library has a helpful Communicate and collaborate online module . Email is the primary way for you to send messages to, and receive information from, the School and our staff. You must use your UQ email address (not a private address) to communicate with staff. You should add a clear subject line, including course code, and a 2-3 word statement. You can send email at any time, however please do not expect responses outside normal working hours (Monday to Friday from ~8am to ~5pm). Emails that constitute bullying, harassment or discrimination against staff contravene the Student Code of Conduct. Emails like this will be reported to the University, and the matter will be pursued as misconduct. Learning activities
